name: charter
description: Know the project + own the owner relationship: gates substantive work on documented quality attributes (web projects get Lighthouse-aligned defaults — CWV, a11y, print CSS, progressive enhancement, components-by-default), maintains a committed Claude-facing roadmap/backlog file (cross-session/engineer coordination), keeps a project map (file→responsibility index) so a session orients from the map instead of re-scanning the tree, treats recorded decisions/ADRs as the alignment anchor (consult before reversing a choice; capture the evident ones when missing) so past choices aren't re-litigated, and records stack notes (languages/frameworks/versions) for modernization context. Detects the project's ESTABLISHED conventions (component/UI library, styling, state, components dir, test framework) from its manifests + structure and surfaces them so new features REUSE what's there rather than introducing a parallel pattern to clean up later — automatic until they're recorded, then quiet. /charter:align reconciles open/proposed work against the recorded decisions + roadmap on demand (clean ≠ correct — surface drift and decision-contradictions before the work is done, not at review). Generates the docs from the codebase/git history when missing, reconciles them when present, and goes quiet once the policy is summarised in CLAUDE.md (claude-companion marker). Surfaces recently-merged commits next to the roadmap so reconciliation is concrete. Documents proportionally to complexity (baseline map + what's-next always; quality attributes for web; decisions/stack by judgment) as one compact brief, not a wall of per-doc prose. For non-technical owners it owns the owner loop: a standing posture (confirm intent in plain language → demonstrate the result back → recap) plus a PreToolUse consent surfacing that reminds — never blocks — before consequential/irreversible actions (paid deps, destructive commands, DB drops, data migrations); and nudges a thin plain-language owner doc layer (README: what it is / how to run it) for bus-factor. Read-only over the project and the consent hook never blocks (the hook detects; the model authors the files and decides).
